channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: How Heated Rivalry's Fandom Became a Battleground of Obsession and Hate
item.description: The rise of the popular TV series *Heated Rivalry*, a hockey-based romance, has resulted in an overwhelming and sometimes toxic fandom. With fans obsessively following the personal lives of the actors...

item.title: Starmer approves US use of UK military bases for defensive operations against Iran
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer, the UK Prime Minister, has authorised the use of British military bases by the United States in a move to counter missile threats from Iran. Despite previously rejecting a similar re...

item.title: AI Map Uncovers Hidden Chemical Changes in Alzheimer’s Brain Beyond Plaques
item.description: Researchers at Rice University have developed the first comprehensive, dye-free molecular map of the Alzheimer’s brain, providing new insights into the disease's spread and impact. Through hyperspectr...

item.title: Ozzy Osbourne's deep connection to Birmingham and his lifelong pride in being a Brummie
item.description: Ozzy Osbourne, the legendary Black Sabbath frontman who passed away last July at the age of 76, was posthumously honoured with a Lifetime Achievement award at the Brit Awards. Sharon Osbourne, speakin...

item.title: Health Crisis from Uranium Mines: Native American Communities Suffer from America's Nuclear Legacy
item.description: Native American communities, particularly the Navajo Nation, have been suffering from the devastating health effects of uranium mining, which supported the U.S. nuclear weapons program. More than 500 ...

item.title: Mexican cartel leader El Mencho dies after military raid in Jalisco following intelligence tip-off
item.description: Nemesio Ruben Oseguera Cervantes, better known as 'El Mencho' and leader of the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG), died following a military operation in the state of Jalisco, Mexico, according to ...

item.title: Exploring Batemates: A Social Platform for LGBTQ+ Men Who Enjoy Masturbation Communities
item.description: Batemates is a social app that provides a virtual space for men, particularly those in the LGBTQ+ community, to engage in group masturbation, or 'bating,' together. Launched in 2024, the app has gaine...

item.title: New York Fashion Brands Balance Minimalism and Maximalism
item.description: New York has long been synonymous with minimalist fashion, from Calvin Klein's dominance in the 1990s to contemporary brands like Khaite and The Row. However, the current trend toward maximalism, mark...

item.title: The Science of the Quadruple Axel in Figure Skating
item.description: The quadruple axel, once considered an impossible feat in figure skating, has been made reality by Team USA's Ilia Malinin, earning him the title 'Quad God.' The jump requires four and a half rotation...

item.title: Health Risks of Bubble Tea: What New Research Reveals
item.description: Bubble tea, once a niche Taiwanese beverage, has now become a global phenomenon. However, new research raises concerns about the potential health risks associated with the drink. The tapioca pearls, m...

item.title: Belgium and France Target Russian Shadow Fleet in North Sea Operation
item.description: Belgian forces, assisted by the French navy, have seized a suspected Russian shadow fleet oil tanker in the North Sea. The tanker, named Ethera, had long been listed under US, EU, and UK sanctions and...

item.title: Peter Mandelson Escorted by Police from London Home Amid Ongoing Investigation
item.description: Former UK politician and ex-US ambassador Peter Mandelson was seen being escorted by police officers from his home in Camden, north London, on Monday afternoon. The 72-year-old was led away by plain-c...

item.title: Mould and water leaks prompt partial closure of cancer ward at Glasgow's Queen Elizabeth University Hospital
item.description: Concerns are growing at Glasgow's Queen Elizabeth University Hospital (QEUH) following the discovery of mould and water ingress in a cancer ward. The issues have led to the partial closure of the adul...

item.title: Brazilian Researchers Create Honey Infused with Cocoa Shells for Health Benefits
item.description: Researchers at the State University of Campinas (UNICAMP) in São Paulo, Brazil, have developed a new product that blends native bee honey with cocoa bean shells, turning it into a chocolate-infused ho...

item.title: Queen Camilla Meets French Rape Survivor Gisèle Pelicot to Discuss Her Memoir
item.description: Queen Camilla met with French rape survivor Gisèle Pelicot at her official London residence to discuss Pelicot's new memoir, 'Hymn to Life: Shame Has to Change Sides.' Pelicot, who gained internationa...

item.title: Inquiry Reveals Nottingham Triple Killer Viewed Shooting Videos and Had History of Mental Health Issues
item.description: A public inquiry into the Nottingham attacks has revealed that triple killer Valdo Calocane had been watching videos of mass shootings online prior to his crimes. Calocane, diagnosed with paranoid sch...

item.title: Violence erupts across Mexico following death of Jalisco cartel leader
item.description: At least 25 members of Mexico's National Guard have been killed in widespread violence following the death of Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes, widely known as 'El Mencho,' the leader of the Jalisco New Gen...

item.title: Tourette’s advocate apologises after involuntary racial slur during BAFTAs ceremony
item.description: A Tourette’s campaigner, John Davidson MBE, has said he is “deeply mortified” after involuntarily shouting a racial slur during the 2026 BAFTAs ceremony. Davidson, who lives with severe Tourette syndr...

item.title: Man Arrested in Connection to 2021 Murder of Teenager in Front of His Mother
item.description: A 26-year-old man has been arrested on suspicion of murder in relation to the tragic killing of 16-year-old Camron Smith in July 2021. Camron was fatally stabbed in front of his mother when a group of...

item.title: RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus hit by suspected drone attack, no casualties reported
item.description: RAF Akrotiri, a British air base in Cyprus, was struck by a suspected drone attack at midnight local time, the UK Ministry of Defence (MoD) confirmed. Fortunately, no casualties were reported in the i...
